style: Dry red, pale ruby/garnet colour, high acidity, low to medium tannin, red fruit primary with earthy/tertiary notes.

nose: Possible earthy or tertiary notes (forest floor, mushroom)
conclusions: Drink now or age 3-7 years depending on vintage

common_student_errors: Assuming pale colour means low quality or weak wine
Overcalling tannin because of acidity perception
Calling earthy/mushroom notes a fault instead of style characteristic
Missing the balance between acidity and structure

misconceptions_addressed.misconception: Deep colour always equals high quality
misconceptions_addressed.correction: Pinot Noir's pale colour is varietal characteristic; quality comes from balance, complexity, and length

misconceptions_addressed.misconception: High acidity and high tannin go together
misconceptions_addressed.correction: Pinot Noir demonstrates that high acidity can coexist with low-medium tannin
Forest floor, mushroom, and tertiary characters are positive indicators of complexity and bottle age

coaching_hints: Focus on balance, complexity, and length rather than colour intensity.
Never infer structure from colour alone.
Can you identify red fruit under the earthy characters?
What indicates quality in a light-coloured red?

causal_reasoning_paths.effect: Pale colour and lower tannin extraction
High acidity retention and red fruit (not black fruit)
causal_reasoning_paths.example_in_wine: Cherry and raspberry rather than plum or blackberry

causal_reasoning_paths.effect: Tertiary development of earthy, gamey, forest floor notes
causal_reasoning_paths.example_in_wine: Mushroom and leather aromas in matured wine
